title: Word Formation System
description: Letters float through 3D space, attach into syllables, syllables attach into words, and finished words gain sound, meaning, and category labels.

title: Sentence Construction Machine
description: Nouns, verbs, adjectives, and prepositions travel on colored tracks, snap into sentence slots, and form readable sentences.

title: Grammar as a Force Field
description: Words behave like particles in a grammar field, where compatible words attract, incompatible words repel, and stable sentence chains glow.

title: Meaning Network
description: Words appear as nodes connected by relation links, and activating one word sends pulses through related meanings.

title: Language Evolution Over Time
description: Words drift across generations, split into descendants, merge as cognates, borrow across language regions, and change pronunciation.
